_comment_purpose: Source-of-truth template for the user-level Cursor MCP config. Rendered by scripts/render-mcp-config.ps1 (Windows) and scripts/agent-bootstrap.sh (Linux Cloud Agent). The 1Password references in this file are resolved by 'op inject' against the EquipQR Agents 1Password vault using the OP service account token (headless) or interactive op signin (local Windows). The 7 plugin-managed MCPs (supabase, vercel, figma, betterstack, context7, browser, app-control) live in the Cursor projects mcps folder and are auto-loaded by Cursor's plugin system; they are intentionally absent here.
_comment_required_op_items: This template assumes the following items exist in the EquipQR Agents 1Password vault (UUID tgo2m6qbct5otqeqirjocn3joa): 'todiagram' (field: credential), 'github-read' (field: credential), 'github-write' (field: credential, used by the github-write MCP entry for mutating GitHub operations - dedup'd 2026-04-20), 'gcp-viewer' (handled by render-mcp-config.ps1, not via op inject). The 'gcp-editor' item is vestigial under the hybrid impersonation pattern - the gcloud-write MCP entry reuses the gcp-viewer JSON and impersonates the editor SA via CLOUDSDK_AUTH_IMPERSONATE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT, so gcp-editor is no longer required by the renderer (recommend revocation in a follow-up CR after 30 days of green doctor runs). If any required item/field is missing, 'op inject' will fail with a clear error and render-mcp-config.ps1 will abort.
_comment_playwright_config: The Playwright MCP --config path uses Cursor's dollar-curly userHome and pathSeparator placeholders so the rendered config is portable across users and operating systems. Cursor's MCP loader (not op-inject) expands these at MCP-server launch time; op-inject only touches secret-reference URIs and leaves dollar-curly placeholders intact. Do not put a literal dollar-curly with three-dots-as-name in this file - newer op-inject parses it as a template variable and rejects dot as an invalid character. Also do not put a literal secret-reference URI scheme (the four chars o, p, colon, slash, slash) in narrative text - op-inject will try to consume the rest of the line as a vault/item/field path.

mcpServers._comment_warning_github_write: WRITE ACCESS - github-write omits the X-MCP-Readonly header so this entry can mutate GitHub state. Only invoke when explicitly authorizing a write action (creating/editing issues, PRs, comments, releases, secrets, etc.). The PAT scope set on the github-write 1Password item should match what the AI Administrator orchestration in issue 644 expects: full repo + admin:repo_hook + workflow + secrets:write + admin:org if applicable. Rotate per the 90-day cadence in .cursor/skills/secrets-rotation/SKILL.md.
mcpServers._comment_datadog: Datadog MCP intentionally disabled to reduce costs. To re-enable: restore the 'datadog' entry below (see git history of this file), update the URL site (mcp.{site}.datadoghq.com), confirm the API key value (NOT the key UUID) is in the 'datadog-prod' item (api_key field) of the EquipQR Agents 1Password vault (UUID tgo2m6qbct5otqeqirjocn3joa), then run scripts/render-mcp-config.ps1. Avoid restating the secret-reference URI scheme inline here; op-inject would otherwise consume the rest of this comment as a vault path and abort the render.
mcpServers._comment_warning_gcloud_write: WRITE ACCESS via impersonation - gcloud-write reuses the viewer SA JSON for bootstrap auth and sets CLOUDSDK_AUTH_IMPERSONATE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT to the editor SA email, so every gcloud command launched through this MCP automatically impersonates the editor SA without a static second key. Matches the AGENTS.md line 25 hybrid impersonation pattern. The viewer SA holds roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator on the editor SA (project-scoped to equipqr-prod) so impersonation works without keys. Only invoke when explicitly authorizing a write/admin action.
